zotero.abstractNote: Gaussian processes (GPs) are powerful tools for molecular property prediction due to their ability to provide well-calibrated uncertainty estimates. However, standard GPs often struggle with high-dimensional molecular representations and the limited data regimes typical of drug discovery. Deep kernel learning (DKL) addresses this by learning a feature mapping using a neural network, but it is prone to overfitting in low-data settings. In this work, we propose a meta-learning approach to train adaptive deep kernel Gaussian processes. By leveraging a large collection of related molecular property prediction tasks, our method learns a prior over the parameters of the deep kernel, which can be rapidly adapted to new tasks with few data points. We demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on several molecular property prediction benchmarks, while providing reliable uncertainty estimates.
zotero.conferenceName: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Eleventh International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)
